ogTitle: Dolphin Point vs Dombarton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dolphin Point and Dombarton.
heading: Dolphin Point vs Dombarton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dolphin Point, New South Wales and Dombarton, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Dolphin Point has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Dombarton (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dolphin Point or Dombarton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dolphin Point has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Dombarton scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dolphin Point scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Dombarton scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dolphin Point or Dombarton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Dolphin Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
